mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls indicates persistent labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in more than 55% of instances, or the Rank IC between PAYEMS changes and 20-day returns is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy sharply elevates real discount rates and debt-service costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in less than 55% of triggered instances, or the conditional mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in retail sales and month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate jointly signal collapsing aggregate demand and labor market stress, amplifying recession risk and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power (partial F-test p > 0.05) beyond individual RSAFS and UNRATE effects.

mechanism_narrative: Following two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production, a subsequent month-over-month rebound in industrial production signals the end of a supply-side contraction and triggers a sharp recovery in corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rebounds after two consecutive monthly declines
failure_condition: Post-shock rebound signals in INDPRO fail to generate positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the mean forward return following the trigger is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration that undermines consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following UNRATE jumps > 0.2pp are not negative in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the Sharpe ratio of the conditional short signal is above -0.3.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above the trailing twelve-month CPI inflation trend, positive real policy rates contract liquidity and squeeze corporate profit margins, jointly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of elevated FEDFUNDS relative to CPIAUCSL growth fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term between the two series exhibits a Rank IC above -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP signals broad-based aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate sales and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following positive quarterly GDPC1 growth are not positive in more than 55% of instances, or the Rank IC between quarterly GDPC1 changes and 60-day returns is below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts above 1.5 million units signal robust residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials, appliances, and credit, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns when HOUST exceeds 1500 are not positive in more than 55% of instances, or the conditional mean return is not significantly above the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).
